title: Focus Mode Example
text:
Preface
This is a minimal example of the Focus Mode extension for Quarto Book.
Two keyboard-driven modes that work on top of any Quarto Book HTML output:

Focus Mode (keyboard shortcut f) hides the sidebar and margin TOC so the content takes center stage — great for reading without distractions and bigger images. To get back the TOC, press (keyboard shortcut t) or click the floating button in the bottom-left corner.
Presentation Mode (keyboard shortcut p) splits the page into one slide per section or subsection.

Navigate forward and backward with PageDown / PageUp within the same chapter, and → / ← to move between chapters. A progress bar shows where you are in the book.

title: Focus Mode
text:
How it works
Press f to enter Focus Mode. The sidebar and margin TOC disappear, and the content recenters for comfortable reading.
Press t to bring the navigation back.
The floating button in the bottom-left corner does the same thing with a click.
If text looks too small or too large, use Ctrl + to zoom in and Ctrl - to zoom out (Ctrl 0 resets the zoom level).
Focus Mode adds the class book-focus-mode to <body>. The sidebar (#quarto-sidebar) and margin TOC (#quarto-margin-sidebar) are hidden with CSS, and the main content column is recentered.
The selected state is saved in localStorage, so it persists as you navigate between chapters.

title: Focus Mode
Focus Mode
text:
Dark and light theme
If your book is configured with a dark theme (e.g. theme: [flatly, darkly]), you can switch between schemes with:

d — Dark Mode
l — Light Mode

title: Focus Mode
text:
Chapter navigation
To move to the previous or next chapter, use ← and →.

title: Presentation Mode
text:
Section navigation
Press p to enter Presentation Mode. The page is split into one slide per section — every heading level becomes its own slide.
Navigate with:
A progress bar at the bottom of the screen shows how far you are in the book, and a small badge in the bottom-right corner shows your position within the current page (e.g. 3 / 12).
Press p again to exit.
This is the first subsection of this chapter. Press PageDown to move to the next one.

title: Presentation Mode
text:
Introduction
Each heading level becomes its own slide. This is the ### Introduction subsection of ## Section navigation (Section navigation).


Details
This is another subsection. Notice how the content above is hidden — only the current section is visible.

title: Presentation Mode
text:
Prelude
If the page has content before the first heading (like a short intro paragraph), that content is shown as the first slide.

title: Presentation Mode
text:
Combining modes
Focus Mode and Presentation Mode are independent. You can activate both at the same time: press f then p for a clean, distraction-free presentation.

title: Installation
text:
Requirements
Add the extension to your Quarto project:
Then enable the filter in _quarto.yml:
Render as usual:
The extension automatically detects whether the page uses the Quarto Book sidebar layout. The floating button hides itself on pages that do not.

title: Installation
text:
Quarto ≥ 1.4
HTML book output (project: type: book)

title: Keyboard shortcuts
text:
Key
Action




f
Enter Focus Mode


t
Return to TOC


p
Toggle Presentation Mode


d / l
Dark / Light theme


→ / ←
Next / Previous chapter


PageDown / PageUp
Next / Previous section (Presentation Mode)
